pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh and verifier.py confirms this filename is checked
Code analysis of export_result.sh and verifier.py confirms PNG file checking
Task description specifies Hub_Customer, Hub_Order, Hub_Product; verifier.py lines 52-59 check for minimum 3 hubs
Task description specifies Link_Order_Customer and Link_Order_Line; verifier.py lines 62-70 check for minimum 2 links
Task description specifies Sat_Customer, Sat_Order, Sat_Product; verifier.py lines 73-81 check for minimum 3 satellites
Web search confirms these are standard Data Vault 2.0 metadata columns; verifier.py lines 95-98 checks for these columns
Hex color #dae8fc converts to RGB(218, 232, 252) = light blue; verifier.py lines 104-106 checks for hub color
Hex color #f8cecc converts to RGB(248, 206, 204) = light pink/red; verifier.py lines 111-113 checks for link color
Hex color #fff2cc converts to RGB(255, 242, 204) = light yellow; verifier.py lines 118-120 checks for satellite color

pi_items.source: Web search confirms Northwind Customers table schema matches task.json description and setup_task.sh
Web search confirms Northwind Orders table schema matches task specification
Web search confirms Northwind Products table schema matches task specification
Web search on Data Vault 2.0 standards confirms Hub column requirements
Web search on Data Vault 2.0 standards confirms Link column requirements; also includes foreign hash keys
Web search on Data Vault 2.0 standards confirms Satellite column requirements

privileged_info_summary: The task is a Data Vault 2.0 modeling exercise using the Northwind sample database. Northwind is a real Microsoft sample database containing tables for Customers (CustomerID, CompanyName, ContactName, Address, City, Country), Orders (OrderID, CustomerID, OrderDate, ShipCity, ShipCountry), and Products (ProductID, ProductName, UnitPrice, Discontinued). The task requires creating three Hub entities (Customer, Order, Product), two Links (Order-Customer, Order-Product), and three Satellites with standard Data Vault 2.0 columns: HashKey, LoadDate, and RecordSource (plus HashDiff for satellites). The color scheme specified (light blue for hubs, light pink/red for links, light yellow for satellites) is custom to this task and verified as correct for draw.io visualization. All metadata claims are accurate and internally consistent with Data Vault 2.0 standards.
